free_text: Slow Neutron Velocity Spectrometer Studies. III. I,
Os, Co, Tl, Cb, Ge
The Columbia University slow neutron
velocity spectrometer.
Compiler's comment: Plots were digitized vs. neutron
time of flight in  microsec/meter and converted to
energy by compiler using the relation
E = (72.3/t)**2 eV which gives the neutron
energy for a timing t, microsec/meter from. Formula
was taken from Phys. Rev. 71, 165 (1947) and verified
using the method of visual inspection. Transmission
data are more reliable, while cross section scale is
less obvious.
Translated from SCISRS.
Converted to reaction formalism.
BP: Updated to new date formats, lower
case. Corrections according last EXFOR rules and
Dict. Corrected title, added doi, method and sample.
Digitized the data. Subentries 13-37 were added.
12.4 g/cm2. A spectrographic analysis of the sample
showed less than 0.5%t P, Si, Mn; 0.05% Mg, Ca, Fe,
Cu, B, Al; 0.005% Ni, As; traces of Be, Cr, Mo, Ag,
In, Ge, Sn, and Pb.
Measured with good resolution above 0.5 eV.
Outside resonances, the cross section from the
experimental transmission T by using the formula
T=exp(-n sigma), where n is the number of atoms per
cm2 in the sample. In the thermal region cross
section: sigma = 6.7 + 6.4*E**(-1/2).
No details on sources of uncertainties.

analysis of the sample showed less than 0.1% of Fe;

and Cu.
Measurements as obtained by using broad
resolution over a wide energy range.

Fig. 1, page 174; values converted by
compiler as described under the compiler's comment.
A 17.3 g/cm2 sample of metal powder. A spectrographic
analysis of the sample showed less than 1% Si; 0.5%
Ca, Ti, Fe, Al, Bi; 0.05% Cr, Mn, Pt, Cu, Au, Pb;
0.005% Ag; traces of Zr, V, Co, Ni, Pa, Pd, Ir, Sn,
and P.
The transmission of this sample was
investigated using low resolution over the full
timing range.
Outside resonances, the cross section from the
experimental transmission T by using the formula
T=exp(-n sigma), where n is the number of atoms per
cm2 in the sample.  In the thermal region cross
section: sigma = 15 + 2.7*E**(-1/2).
No details on sources of uncertainties.

A sample containing 52.09 g/cm2 of the material. A
spectrographic analysis of the sample showed less
than 0.05% Ca, Pb; 0.005% Fe, Cu, Al; traces of Mg,
Cr, Mn, Cd, Si. For all other metals the prominent
lines were not apparent.
The transmission of this sample was
investigated using broad energy resolution.
Outside resonances, the cross section from the
experimental transmission T by using the formula
T=exp(-n sigma), where n is the number of atoms per
cm2 in the sample.
No details on sources of uncertainties.

A 19.37 g/cm2 disk of Cb metal A spectrographic
analysis of the samples showed less than 1% Tl; 0.5%
Zr; 0.05% Ca, Y, Zn; 0.005% Be, Sc, Ti, Ni, Rh, Pd,
Si; traces of V, Cr, W, Mn, Fe, Ca, Au, Al, Ga, Sn, Bi.
The region above 0.5 eV was then studied with
good resolution. The weak dip at 4.1 eV which can be
attributed to an 0.4 percent Ta impurity.
